analytical_depth.rationale: Excellent synthesis of delivery deterioration with price resistance to identify speculative momentum, though it misses analyzing volume trends specifically on down vs. up days.

logical_consistency.rationale: Conclusions perfectly track the data, though the connection between early April FII selling and mid-April delivery drops could have been explicitly linked.

completeness.rationale: Covers price, volume, and macro well, but completely misses derivatives data (Open Interest, PCR) which is critical for a high-beta F&O stock like NTPC.

actionability.rationale: Provides exact support/resistance levels and clear conditional entry scenarios, though it lacks a defined stop-loss level for the breakout trade scenario.

sector_framework.rationale: Outstanding. Perfectly adapts technical analysis for a regulated utility by incorporating the G-Sec yield spread and debunking the low-beta myth. Perfect score justified.

data_sourcing.rationale: Good use of calculation tools and clear citations, though relying on March MF data for late April price action creates a slight temporal mismatch.

tool_use_discipline.rationale: Log shows excellent discipline: used the calculate tool 4 times for averages/sums and honestly reported the FMP indicator limitation without hallucinating data.

cost_efficiency.rationale: Cost is low ($0.34) and cache hit rate is solid (65.5%), but 17 turns is on the higher side for a single-agent technical scope.

issues.issue: Missing derivatives data (Open Interest, Put-Call Ratio) which is essential for analyzing near-term resistance in a highly liquid F&O stock.
issues.suggestion: Update instructions to require F&O positioning analysis for large-cap stocks with active derivatives markets.

strengths: Exceptional sector-specific adaptation by integrating the G-Sec yield spread into the technical/momentum analysis.
Strong synthesis of price action and delivery percentage to differentiate between genuine accumulation and speculative momentum.
regulated_power.summary: This is a highly sophisticated technical report that brilliantly adapts standard momentum analysis to the realities of a regulated utility. The integration of the G-Sec yield spread and the nuanced interpretation of delivery data at 52-week highs demonstrate institutional-grade analytical depth. The only meaningful gap is the absence of derivatives data (OI/PCR), which would have provided a more complete picture of the resistance at the 52-week high.
